beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make espresso, check messages.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning routine at full energy; Hazel's day always starts with strong coffee and a clear head before deciding how to shape the day ahead.

beats.activity: Commute to Ballard office by car.
beats.reasoning: Standard commute from West Seattle to Ballard startup; Light Rail closure doesn't affect her, and sunny weather makes the drive pleasant.

beats.activity: Work on cloud infrastructure deployment and code review.
beats.reasoning: Focused morning work block before the FIFA match airs; Hazel is skeptical about the hype but curious enough to check it out at a bar later.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales match at a downtown bar with a colleague.
beats.reasoning: Catches the 11am FIFA match live as scheduled; early opening and sunny weather draw crowds; Hazel goes for the social aspect and to humor a coworker's enthusiasm, not from deep fan investment.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at Pike Place Market.
beats.reasoning: Already downtown and sunny; quick, casual lunch at the market before heading back to the office; fits her skeptical, no-fuss eating style.
Return to work for the afternoon; Light Rail closure is irrelevant since she's driving.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: infrastructure debugging and team sync.
beats.reasoning: Solid afternoon work block; Hazel settles into the complex problems that require stubborn focus and espresso fuel.
End-of-day commute; sunny weather and light traffic on the drive home.

beats.activity: Paint in home studio, work on a landscape piece.
beats.reasoning: Golden hour light is perfect for painting; Hazel unwinds after a social and work-heavy day with solitary creative time, her favorite way to end a Monday.
Simple home meal; Hazel values quiet evenings and keeps dinner low-key after a full day.

beats.activity: Read and wind down for bed.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down before sleep; Hazel is already at home and settling into a calm routine for the night.
Full night's rest after a balanced Monday; wakes refreshed the next morning.

diary: Monday started crisp and early—espresso in hand, I was out the door to Ballard by 8:30. The office felt lighter than usual, which I realized was because half the team had ducked out early or shifted their schedule around the World Cup match. I caved at 10:30 and headed downtown with Marcus to catch USA vs Wales at Belltown Pub. The place was already packed, and I got caught up in the energy—something about a sunny Monday and a live match just hits different. Grabbed a quick sandwich at Pike Place after, then back to the grind for debugging and a team sync. The light rail closure didn't affect me today since I drove, but I noticed the traffic was messier than usual. By evening, I was in my studio with my landscape piece, letting the afternoon light pour through the window. Paint, dinner, a book, and bed. A good day—nothing earthshaking, just the right mix of work and breathing room.
thought_process: The World Cup timing threw off my usual rhythm, but I leaned into it instead of fighting it. Marcus mentioned the match casually, and honestly, I needed a break—the morning's code review had been thorny. The weather was too perfect to stay locked in the office anyway. I felt a little guilty about the mid-day detour, but the team was in good shape, and afternoon work proved we made up for it. My mood shifted from task-focused to social to creative as the day unfolded. The light rail closure was background noise; I was grateful my commute route wasn't affected. By evening, painting felt like the natural decompression I needed after the social buzz of the pub. It was a balanced day—not forced, just right.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
